articles.title: After Virginia Redistricting Map Is Tossed, Democrats Search Desperately for a Response
articles.summary: A conversation involving House members from Virginia and the top House Democrat reflected the fury and desperation that has gripped the party after Friday’s ruling in the state.

articles.title: Lebanese Mourn Eight Members of One Family Killed in Israeli Strike
articles.summary: Israel has escalated attacks on Lebanon since Thursday, further unraveling a truce in its war with Hezbollah. Among those killed on Saturday was a 6-month-old infant, relatives said.

articles.title: Trump Administration Is Considering Pausing the Federal Gas Tax to Lower Prices, Energy Secretary Sa
articles.summary: The tax is a little over 18 cents a gallon for gasoline. With the average national price above $4.50, the measure will provide little relief.

articles.title: A chocolate laboratory in Italy will be good for chocolate eaters — and farmers
articles.summary: The chocolate biz is raising the bar with a lab to assess cacao beans from around the world. (Talk about a sweet gig!) Consumers and farmers stand to benefit from the "Standard of Excellence" program.

articles.title: Keir Starmer's party lost big in U.K. local elections. Here's what comes next
articles.summary: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Labour party suffered major losses in local elections held across Britain last week. So far, Starmer has rejected calls for his resignation.

articles.title: Temple Israel rebuilds after terror attack
articles.summary: Two months ago, a gunman drove through the preschool at Temple Israel, the largest synagogue in Michigan. Every child and teacher survived, but the community is still displaced and navigating the trau

articles.title: Happy Mother's Day to the kindest mom. P.S. Your kindness annoyed me when I was a kid
articles.summary: She and her siblings had to make tea for and share food with every visitor. That did not make her happy. Today she has a different perspective. So she finally asked her mom: What made you so kind?

articles.title: Of course we look for ourselves in art — but if we stop there, we're missing out
articles.summary: As I watched the new series, I only cared about Piggy — the thoughtful, smart kid stranded on an island with other boys. That made me think about what we look for in art.

articles.title: How a pill approved 25 years ago transformed cancer treatment
articles.summary: When the Food and Drug Administration approved Gleevec to treat a form of leukemia in 2001, it ushered in a new era in cancer care.

articles.title: Cape Verde: Tiny nation, massive World Cup dream
articles.summary: In Cape Verde, a small island nation off West Africa, World Cup qualification is transforming dreams on and off the pitch.

articles.title: Iran ceasefire tested as cargo ship catches fire after being hit off Qatar's coast
articles.summary: A cargo ship caught fire Sunday after being hit by an unknown projectile off Qatar's coast, the British military said.

articles.title: Democratic Candidates Scramble in Virginia After Court Tosses Map
articles.summary: A court ruling that struck down an election map swiftly ended some Democratic House candidacies and pushed others into much tougher races.

articles.title: Vance or Rubio? Trump Muses on Successor as the ‘Kids’ Fill Bigger Roles.
articles.summary: Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio are boosting their profiles, generating speculation about who’s lining up for the 2028 presidential nomination.

articles.title: One Big Headache for Politicians These Days: a Messy Digital Footprint
articles.summary: As a new generation of chronically online oversharers runs for office, many find themselves tripped up by past statements. Their response? Delete, distance, disavow.

articles.title: Mass Layoffs in Iran as Businesses Buckle Under Wartime Pressures
articles.summary: Iran was already struggling economically before 2026 brought widespread instability. A government-imposed internet shutdown has crippled an entire sector.

articles.title: G.O.P. Fatigue in Iowa Strains the Republican Primary for Governor
articles.summary: Five candidates are battling for the nomination in the June 2 primary. The winner will oppose Rob Sand, a Democrat, in the November election.

articles.title: Protests and boycotts rock prestigious Venice Biennale
articles.summary: The 61st edition of the international art event launched on Saturday in an atmosphere marked by geopolitical strife.

articles.title: Mideast war worsens conflict between elephants and villagers in Sri Lanka
articles.summary: Conflict between farmers and elephants in Sri Lanka has grown worse as food and fuel are scarcer due to war in the Middle East.

articles.title: An expert on Iranian politics reviews the status of negotiations to end the war on Iran
articles.summary: NPR's Elissa Nadworny talks to Mehrzad Boroujerdi of the Missouri University of Science and Technology about the status of the Trump Administration's negotiations to end the war on Iran.

articles.title: Revisiting Guy Goma's iconic accidental BBC interview 20 years ago this week
articles.summary: NPR's Elissa Nadworny talks to Guy Goma who thought he was being interviewed for an IT job at the BBC but instead found himself live on air. His interview 20 years ago became a viral sensation.

articles.title: Moscow marks Victory Day with a Red Square parade under tight security
articles.summary: Security was tight in Moscow as Putin and several foreign leaders attended the parade, even as a U.S.-brokered three-day ceasefire eased concerns about possible Ukrainian attempts to disrupt the festi

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
